_article: XXXIX.2 — cultivators approaching breakthrough. XXXIX.3 — the player intersects trajectories (an elder is nearing breakthrough when the player arrives).
_description: Momentum schema for a cultivator (typically an elder or sect head) approaching a realm breakthrough. This is the canonical 'something is about to happen' trajectory. The cultivator accumulates qi/spirit toward the breakthrough, the backlash risk rises, a window of vulnerability opens (the cultivator cannot be disturbed), and if the breakthrough succeeds the regional balance shifts. If it fails, the cultivator may die or be crippled — and the sect/family dependent on them begins a decline trajectory.

_design_notes.why_this_matters: Wang Lin's own breakthroughs are canon. But the canonical 'elder nearing breakthrough' for Chapter 1 is the Heng Yue patriarch (or a senior elder) whose breakthrough window creates political opportunity (factions maneuver) and danger (the sect is briefly vulnerable). This is perceivable: the elder is in seclusion, the sect is tense, disciples are on high alert, rumors circulate.
_design_notes.coupling: Feeds: sect_momentum (a successful patriarch breakthrough may ascend the sect; a failed one may trigger decline_spiral), faction_grudge_momentum (succession anxiety), rumor_momentum (the seclusion is rumored). Fed by: spirit_vein_momentum (qi density affects accumulation rate and backlash risk).
perceivable_without_notification_example: The sect's main hall is quieter than usual — the patriarch is in seclusion, disciples whisper.
A disciple on the road is turning away visitors 'for the next month' without explaining why.
Spirit density around the sect's peak is noticeably higher (perceivable by a cultivator player's spiritual sense, not a UI bar).
An unusual number of rare herbs have been purchased in the village market — breakthrough preparation.

schema.cultivator_id: string — matches an NPC id (the elder/patriarch)

current_state.progress: float 0.0–1.0 — accumulation toward the breakthrough threshold
current_state.accumulation_rate_per_tick: float — how fast progress is rising (driven by spirit vein qi density, meditation quality, supplements)
current_state.backlash_risk: float 0.0–1.0 — probability the breakthrough will fail catastrophically (driven by progress quality, interruptions, mental state, karmic load)
current_state.vulnerability_window: object — {open: bool, ticks_remaining: int} — when in the final approach, the cultivator cannot be disturbed without risking catastrophic failure
current_state.seclusion_location: string — entity id of the CaveDwelling / hall where the cultivator is sealed
current_state.supplements_remaining: float 0.0–1.0 — pills/herbs/array power supporting the breakthrough

rate_of_change.progress_per_tick: float — primary driver; rises with qi density, falls if supplements run out, halts if interrupted
rate_of_change.backlash_risk_per_tick: float — rises as progress approaches 1.0 (the closer, the more dangerous); rises sharply if interrupted during vulnerability_window; falls slightly with each successful meditation cycle

components.mental_state: float — meditation quality; disturbed by interruptions, sect anxiety, karmic disturbances
components.supplement_burn: float — supplements consumed per tick; when depleted, accumulation_rate drops and backlash_risk rises

drivers: string — e.g. 'heng_yue_patriarch targeting nascent_soul_early, progress 0.78, backlash_risk 0.31, vulnerability_window opens at progress 0.9 (~6000 ticks), supplements at 0.45 (will deplete before completion unless resupplied)'

thresholds.name: vulnerability_window_open
thresholds.consequence: cultivator enters seclusion lockout; cannot be disturbed; the sect is briefly under-stewarded; factions maneuver; external threats are more dangerous (the sect's strongest defender is unavailable). This window is the political opportunity.

thresholds.name: breakthrough_success
thresholds.condition: progress >= 1.0 AND backlash_risk resolved in cultivator's favor
thresholds.consequence: cultivator advances a realm; sect_momentum may trigger ascension; influence_map expands; the cultivator's capability_score rises; a canon-grade memory_ledger entry is written; regional balance shifts.

thresholds.name: breakthrough_failure
thresholds.condition: progress >= 1.0 AND backlash_risk resolved against cultivator OR interruption during vulnerability_window
thresholds.consequence: cultivator is crippled or killed; sect_momentum may trigger decline_spiral or schism (succession crisis); faction_grudge_momentum may ignite; the region's power balance shifts the other way. This is how sects fall in Er Gen canon.

thresholds.name: supplement_exhaustion
thresholds.consequence: accumulation_rate drops sharply; backlash_risk rises; the cultivator must either resupply (delaying, requiring the seclusion to break — dangerous) or push through under-supported (raises failure risk). This is the perceivable 'rare herbs being bought in the market' cue.

interruptible_by: Player or NPC delivers rare supplements (lowers backlash_risk, raises accumulation_rate)
Player or NPC guards the seclusion (prevents interruption during vulnerability_window)
Player or NPC causes interruption (raises backlash_risk sharply; may trigger failure — this is a canon-grade intervention with consequences)
Canon event: karmic disturbance, restriction rupture, ancient god influence (may help or hinder)
Sect faction sabotage (interruption intended to trigger failure — the succession play)
schema.perceivable_without_notification: string — REQUIRED (Art XXXIX.5). e.g. 'The Heng Yue patriarch has not been seen in the sect's main hall for weeks. A disciple at the gate turns visitors away with a strained face. The village herbalist mentions, unprompted, that the sect bought three times the usual spirit-replenishing herbs last month. The air on the sect's peak feels heavier — to a cultivator, unmistakable.' If empty, FAILS the Momentum Test.
schema.compiled_into: settlement_graph (sect node state); feeds sect_momentum, faction_grudge_momentum, rumor_momentum
schema.canon_ref: EXPLICIT — cultivator breakthroughs and their backlash are central Er Gen canon; the specific elder and timing INFERRED (Art XV) for Chapter 1
